essence: Builds the temporary structures that make construction possible, combining strength, balance, and absolute safety discipline.

identity.title: Scaffolder
identity.summary: Erects, modifies, and dismantles scaffolding structures on construction and industrial sites, ensuring safe access for workers at height.

salary.notes: Advanced scaffolders, offshore roles, and industrial projects can exceed £55k–£70k+.

qualifications: CISRS Part 1/Part 2 or Advanced Scaffolder certification
Working at height and site‑safety training
Understanding of load limits, bracing, and structural safety

lifestyle.description: Scaffolders work outdoors on construction, industrial, and maintenance projects, building the access structures that allow other trades to work safely at height. Days involve carrying and assembling tubes, boards, and fittings; climbing structures; reading scaffold plans; and ensuring stability and safety. The work is physically demanding and often performed in all weather conditions. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy physical challenges, teamwork, and working at height with precision and confidence.

habits: Checks fittings and bracing for stability
